markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Prepare Master Spec UserManual wording and appendices entries for future enrichment. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualSpecEnrichmentSeed leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualSpecEnrichmentSeed is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Spec enrichment seed UMSPEC-002 now mirrors runtime non-page freshness verdict coverage and recovery semantics; content proof and adversarial review PASS.

evidence.summary: UMSPEC-002 seed text now names all runtime freshness verdicts and states that pages, tool entries, feature entries, and legacy aliases are checked against the seed corpus.
Content regression proves UMSPEC-002 includes the runtime verdict vocabulary and current recovery semantics; full UserManual content/API/storage suites and cargo check passed.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.
Hubble adversarial review returned PASS; no blocking findings, with one non-blocking future hardening idea to count read receipts on the missing-link path.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-207 prepares a typed, runtime-discoverable UserManual spec-enrichment seed (5 rows, UMSPEC-001..005) grounded in what the MT-193..208 UserManual group actually built, with each seed row's target spec anchor verified to exist and an anchor-existence test enforcing it. The deliverable is real, anchor-accurate, route-wired, and proven green on real PostgreSQL; no placeholders or self-authored-mock proof.
findings: spec_seed.rs:32-110 defines 5 typed SpecEnrichmentSeedRow entries (UMSPEC-001..005), each naming an exact target_module + target_anchor + seed_kind (amend/appendix) + proposed_wording_md grounded in built UserManual surfaces. Not aspirational scaffolding.

All 5 seed anchors verified present in the CURRENT v02.194 bundle (not just cited v02.193): 10.15.8 UserManual migration bridge (10-product-surfaces.md), 2.3.13.11 (02-system-architecture.md), PRIM-UserManual (12-end-of-file-appendices.md), 7.1.1.9 (07-user-experience-and-development.md). The acceptance criterion 'accepted research ideas restated as Handshake-native requirements / anchor-grounded' holds.

UMSPEC-002 wording (spec_seed.rs:55-66) lists the full 12-verdict freshness vocabulary that exactly matches the real FreshnessVerdictKind::as_str() enum in freshness.rs:42-70 (current, stale_content, missing_page, uncovered_surface, dangling_anchor, unseeded_version, missing_tool_entry, stale_tool_entry, missing_feature_entry, stale_feature_entry, missing_legacy_alias, stale_legacy_alias). Seed describes actually-built behavior.

Route is genuinely wired, not stubbed: api/user_manual.rs:68-69 registers GET /usermanual/spec-enrichment-seed; handler at :503-507 returns the real spec_enrichment_seed() rows; surface registered in registry.rs:649. No todo!/unimplemented!/placeholder/deferred markers anywhere in spec_seed.rs or api/user_manual.rs.

Proof ran green on real PostgreSQL (127.0.0.1:5544, isolated schemas): 'test mt207_spec_seed_anchors_exist_in_current_bundle ... ok' (content suite 12 passed) and 'test mt204_freshness_docs_name_every_verdict_kind ... ok' in the recovery green logs. RED log red-mt204-freshness-docs-verdicts.log shows the test genuinely fails when wording drifts, then green after fix — real RED->GREEN.
Self-cert: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER, completed_by=null (external validator signs COMPLETED). Not malformed.

Non-blocking aside (paperwork only, not a fail per brief): the anchor test in user_manual_content_tests.rs:588 hardcodes master-spec-v02.193 while current bundle is v02.194; anchors resolve in both today, so behavior is correct, but the test would SKIP on a future bundle bump. Stale spec pointer is explicitly a non-blocking concern.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
